column1: # ZAP baseline scanner — per-rule overrides for the unauthenticated boot
# We FAIL on the rules that genuinely matter for an internet-facing app and
# IGNORE the noise that always fires on an SPA + minimal-API surface
# (ZAP doesn't see auth endpoints behind credentials in baseline mode).
# IGNORED — not actionable in baseline (unauth, no-TLS) mode

column3: Storable + Cacheable Content (we set Cache-Control on auth responses; static SPA assets are cacheable on purpose)
Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments (Vite source-map comments only present when ASPNETCORE_ENVIRONMENT=Development)
Cookie No HttpOnly Flag (XSRF-TOKEN is intentionally JS-readable for double-submit; baseline cannot distinguish that cookie from session cookies)
Cookie Without Secure Flag (CI scan target is plain HTTP; production always sits behind nginx TLS where cookies become Secure)
Cookie without SameSite Attribute (same caveat — cookies are SameSite=Strict in production-flagged code paths)
CSP: style-src unsafe-inline (intentional — Sonner/Radix/Framer/Vaul inject nonce-less stylesheets at runtime; documented in ARCHITECTURE.md → Security → CSP)
Timestamp Disclosure - Unix (false-positive on source-map references inside the JS bundle; not a runtime timestamp)
Modern Web Application (informational — ZAP can't crawl an SPA behind client-side routing in baseline mode)

column1: # Explicit FAILs — must always block CI

column3: Content Security Policy (CSP) Header Not Set
Insufficient Site Isolation Against Spectre Vulnerability
